about: Igulu Foundation is a non-governmental organization in Jinja, Uganda, dedicated to supporting vulnerable children, single mothers, and sustainable community development.

aboutPage.title: About Igulu Foundation
aboutPage.content: Founded with a passion for humanitarian service, Igulu Foundation has been at the forefront of community transformation in Jinja. We believe that every individual deserves a chance at a better life, and we work tirelessly to provide the necessary support and resources to make this a reality.
aboutPage.mission: To provide education, healthcare, and economic empowerment to vulnerable children and marginalized women through sustainable community-led initiatives.
aboutPage.vision: A thriving Ugandan community where every child is educated and every woman is economically self-reliant.

services.name: Education Sponsorship
services.description: We support children from poor backgrounds with school fees and materials.
services.content: Education is the key to breaking the cycle of poverty. Our sponsorship program covers tuition, uniforms, and stationery for over 100 children in Jinja.

services.name: Children's Home
services.description: Providing a safe haven for orphaned and abandoned children.
services.content: Our home provides more than just shelter; it provides a family. We offer nutrition, medical care, and emotional support in a loving environment.

programs.name: Education Sponsorship
programs.content: Our Education Sponsorship program identifies talented but underprivileged children and ensures they stay in school. We partner with local schools to monitor their progress and provide additional tutoring where needed.

programs.name: Sustainable Agriculture
programs.content: We train local farmers in sustainable techniques to increase crop yields and ensure food security for their families. Our demonstration farm serves as a learning hub for the entire community.

team.name: Musasizi Hamuza
team.bio: Hamuza has over 10 years of experience in community development and is passionate about child welfare.

team.name: Nabirye Sarah
team.bio: Sarah manages our day-to-day operations and ensures all programs are meeting their targets.

news.title: Our lovely goat Kiwaka gave birth to two male kids!
news.summary: Exciting news from our farm! We have two new additions to our livestock family.
news.content: We are thrilled to announce that Kiwaka, one of our favorite goats, successfully gave birth to two healthy male kids this morning. This is a great boost for our sustainable agriculture project, which helps provide milk and income for our children's home.

news.title: Hashim's Successful Palate Surgery
news.summary: A life-changing surgery for one of our children.
news.content: Thanks to our generous donors, Hashim was able to undergo surgery for his cleft palate. The procedure was a success, and he is now recovering well. This surgery will allow him to eat and speak properly, giving him a much brighter future.

reviews.author: John Doe
reviews.text: Igulu Foundation is doing amazing work in Jinja. I've seen firsthand the impact of their education program.
